intended_use: The Siemens Biograph Horizon systems are combined X-Ray Computed Tomography (CT) and Positron Emission Tomography (PET) scanners that provide registration and fusion of high resolution physiologic and
intended_use_full: The Siemens Biograph Horizon systems are combined X-Ray Computed Tomography (CT) and Positron Emission Tomography (PET) scanners that provide registration and fusion of high resolution physiologic and anatomic information. These systems are intended to be utilized by appropriately trained health care professionals to aid in detecting, localizing, diagnosing, staging and restaging of lesions, tumors, disease and organ function for the evaluation of diseases and disorders such as, but not limited to, cardiovascular disease, neurological disorders and cancer. The images produced by the system can also be used by the physician to aid in radiotherapy treatment planning and interventional radiology procedures. This CT system can be used for low dose lung cancer screening in high risk populations.
ground_truth: NEMA NU2-2018 standard phantom testing; simulation studies
other_metrics: Resolution (transverse FWHM @ 1 cm ≤4.7mm; axial FWHM @ 1 cm ≤5.0mm); Sensitivity @435 keV ≥5.8 cps/kBq (≥10.9 cps/kBq for TrueV); Count Rate peak NECR ≥78 kcps (≥135 kcps for TrueV); Scatter Fraction ≤40%; Mean bias ≤+/-6%; Image Quality contrast 10%-65% with background variability ≤10%; Co-Registration Accuracy max error ≤5mm; OncoFreeze AI SUVmax +27%±22%, SUVmean +26%±22%, Volume -31%±19%; FlowMotion AI bed range identification 87.1%-100%; QualityGuard time savings up to 30 minutes/day; Whole Body Scatter Correction difference from ground truth -2% to -3%
